description: Epic 3.5 — Competitive Analysis Retrofit: unify dependency taxonomy, add package registry metadata, git-based convention trends, wrapper/facade detection, function parameter extraction, and heuristic fallbacks for all detectors

userStories.title: Unify DependencyDomain taxonomy in seshat-core
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a single DependencyDomain enum in seshat-core replacing both the 8-variant DependencyDomain in seshat-detectors and the 12-variant DependencyCategory in seshat-scanner so that domain classification has one source of truth.
acceptanceCriteria: Single DependencyDomain enum defined in crates/seshat-core/src/dependency.rs with 12 variants: Http, WebFramework, Logging, Testing, Validation, Serialization, Database, Cli, AsyncRuntime, Crypto, Utilities, Unknown
Derives: Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, Serialize, Deserialize with #[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
Old DependencyDomain enum deleted from crates/seshat-detectors/src/dependency_usage.rs
Old DependencyCategory enum deleted from crates/seshat-scanner/src/manifest.rs
DeclaredDependency.category field in manifest.rs uses unified type from seshat-core
All classify_*() functions in dependency_usage.rs and categorize_*_dep() functions in manifest.rs return unified DependencyDomain
Mapping conflicts resolved: web frameworks (actix-web, express, flask, django, axum, rocket) → WebFramework; HTTP clients (reqwest, axios, httpx) → Http
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add package_metadata SQLite table and PackageRegistryClient trait
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database schema and trait definition for package registry metadata so that client implementations and integration can build on them.
acceptanceCriteria: New migration V3__package_metadata.sql creates package_metadata table: (name TEXT, registry TEXT, categories TEXT, keywords TEXT, description TEXT, fetched_at INTEGER, PRIMARY KEY (name, registry))
PackageRegistryClient trait defined in crates/seshat-scanner/src/registry.rs with fn fetch_metadata(&self, package_name: &str) -> Result<PackageMetadata, RegistryError>
PackageMetadata struct defined with fields: name, registry, categories (Vec<String>), keywords (Vec<String>), description (Option<String>)
RegistryError type defined using thiserror
Storage repository method to upsert and query package_metadata rows
Cache TTL constant: 30 days — stale entries re-fetched on next scan
ureq crate added to seshat-scanner dependencies
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Foundation for US-003. Does NOT implement the HTTP clients yet — just the trait, types, migration, and storage. Note: migration is V2 (not V3 as originally specified in AC) because V2 must come before V3 per US-005 ordering requirement.

userStories.title: Implement CratesIo, Npm, and PyPI registry clients
userStories.description: As a developer, I want concrete PackageRegistryClient implementations for crates.io, npm, and PyPI so that package metadata can be fetched from all three registries.
acceptanceCriteria: CratesIoClient implementation: GET https://crates.io/api/v1/crates/{name} → extract categories[].slug and keywords[]
NpmClient implementation: GET https://registry.npmjs.org/{name} → extract keywords[]
PyPIClient implementation: GET https://pypi.org/pypi/{name}/json → extract classifiers[] and keywords
User-Agent header set per API policies: seshat/{version}
Timeout: 5 seconds per request; network errors → graceful RegistryError return
Unit tests with mock HTTP responses (no real network calls in tests)
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 for trait and types.

userStories.title: Category-to-DependencyDomain mapping rules and three-tier lookup
userStories.description: As a developer, I want ~30 category/classifier-to-DependencyDomain mapping rules and the three-tier lookup logic so that registry metadata replaces hardcoded package lists.
acceptanceCriteria: Category/classifier → DependencyDomain mapping rules (~30 total) in registry_mapping.rs
Three-tier lookup function: (1) SQLite cache → (2) registry API fetch + cache → (3) hardcoded fallback with lower confidence
Existing classify_*() / categorize_*_dep() functions preserved as fallback tier
Known-library matches remain high confidence; registry-inferred matches have normal confidence; fallback has lower confidence
Network errors → graceful fallback to hardcoded mapping, no crash
Unit tests for each tier: cache hit, cache miss + fetch, network failure → fallback
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-002 and US-003. Integrates registry clients into the classification pipeline.

userStories.title: Git file dates collection with gix
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Seshat to collect last git commit date for each file during scan so that convention trend detection can determine Rising/Stable/Declining.
acceptanceCriteria: gix crate added to seshat-scanner with max-performance-safe feature
New function collect_git_file_dates(repo_root: &Path) -> Result<HashMap<PathBuf, i64>, ScanError> in crates/seshat-scanner/src/git_dates.rs
Single commit walk from HEAD — O(commits), NOT per-file git log
Each file mapped to its most recent commit's Unix timestamp
New migration V2__add_file_dates.sql: ALTER TABLE files_ir ADD COLUMN last_commit_date INTEGER
FileIRRepository::upsert() signature updated to accept last_commit_date: Option<i64> as separate parameter (ProjectFile struct NOT modified)
New trait method: fn get_file_dates_by_branch(&self, branch_id: &BranchId) -> Result<HashMap<String, Option<i64>>, StorageError>
Non-git directories: return empty HashMap, no error — all dates stored as NULL
Scan orchestrator calls collect_git_file_dates() after discover_files() and passes dates to upsert
Unit test: temp git repo with commits → verify correct dates collected
Unit test: non-git directory → empty HashMap
Unit test: empty repo → empty HashMap
gix repo handle properly scoped — dropped after commit walk completes
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Migration V2 must come before V3 (package metadata). Does not depend on US-001–US-004.

userStories.title: Convention trend computation with P90 percentile
userStories.description: As a developer, I want each detected convention to have a trend indicator (Rising/Stable/Declining/Unknown) so that AI agents know whether to adopt or avoid a pattern.
acceptanceCriteria: Trend enum in seshat-core/src/knowledge.rs: Rising, Stable, Declining, Unknown with #[serde(rename_all = "snake_case")]
DetectionConfig extended with trend_rising_days: u32 (default 90) and trend_stable_days: u32 (default 365)
New function compute_trend(file_dates: &[Option<i64>], config: &DetectionConfig) -> Trend in seshat-detectors/src/confidence.rs
P90 percentile calculation: sort valid dates ascending, take index ceil(N * 0.9) - 1
Mapping: P90 < trend_rising_days days ago → Rising, < trend_stable_days → Stable, else → Declining, no valid dates → Unknown
AggregatedConvention struct extended with trend: Trend field
aggregate_findings() accepts new parameter file_dates: &HashMap<String, Option<i64>> — computes trend per convention group
Trend stored in KnowledgeNode.ext_data as {"trend": "rising"|"stable"|"declining"|"unknown"} — merged with existing ext_data
Unit tests at threshold boundaries: 89 days (Rising), 90 days (Stable), 364 days (Stable), 365 days (Declining), 366 days (Declining)
Unit test: empty dates → Unknown; all None → Unknown; single date
P90 computation does not hold full file list in memory longer than needed
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-005 for git file dates.

userStories.title: Wrapper/facade convention detection via import graph
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the dependency usage detector to detect wrapper/facade patterns structurally via import graph analysis so that direct usage of wrapped external dependencies is flagged as a convention violation.
acceptanceCriteria: ConventionDetector trait extended with default method: fn detect_cross_file(&self, files: &[ProjectFile]) -> Vec<ConventionFinding> (default: empty Vec)
run_all_detectors() in pipeline updated: after per-file detection, runs detect_cross_file() for each detector and merges findings

DependencyUsageDetector overrides detect_cross_file() with wrapper detection: for each external dep, find direct importers and wrapper candidates, if consumer_count > direct_importer_count → wrapper convention
Direct importers when wrapper exists: follows_convention = false, description: 'Use {wrapper_module} for {external_dep} operations'
No hardcoded directory names — purely import graph structural analysis
Import resolution heuristics: Rust (crate::/super::/self:: = internal), TS/JS (starts with ./ or ../ = internal), Python (module path matches project file = internal)
Wrapper file itself NOT flagged as violating its own convention
Works for all 4 supported languages (Rust, TypeScript, JavaScript, Python)
Unit test: Python wrapper pattern (wrapper used by 5 files, 2 direct users → 2 violations)
Unit test: TypeScript wrapper pattern (wrapper used by 4 files, 1 direct user → 1 violation)
Unit test: no wrapper exists → no convention detected
Unit test: wrapper used by minority (<50%) → no convention established
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Independent of registry and trend stories. Extends ConventionDetector trait with backward-compatible default method.

userStories.title: Add function parameter extraction to all 4 Tree-sitter parsers
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the Function struct to include parameter names extracted by all 4 Tree-sitter parsers so that the naming detector can analyze parameter naming conventions.
acceptanceCriteria: Function struct in seshat-core/src/ir.rs extended with parameters: Vec<String> field
Rust tree-sitter parser extracts parameter names from function_item and method_definition nodes
TypeScript tree-sitter parser extracts parameter names from function_declaration, arrow_function, method_definition nodes
JavaScript tree-sitter parser extracts parameter names (same node types as TS)
Python tree-sitter parser extracts parameter names from function_definition nodes (excluding self/cls)
Existing tests updated — make_project_file() test helper produces parameters: vec![] by default
New tests: parse fixture files, verify parameter names extracted correctly for each language
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Independent of other stories. Extends IR struct — all existing code gets empty vec by default.

userStories.title: Parameter naming analysis in naming detector
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the naming detector to analyze function parameter naming conventions so that AI agents follow consistent parameter naming across the project.
acceptanceCriteria: Naming detector in naming.rs analyzes parameter name case patterns (snake_case, camelCase) per language
Language-aware weighting: Rust parameter naming weighted lower (compiler/clippy convention), JS/TS/Python weighted higher
New tests: verify parameter naming analysis produces correct findings for each language
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Depends on US-008 for parameter extraction.

userStories.title: Heuristic fallbacks for error handling and logging detectors
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the error handling and logging detectors to identify unknown libraries via heuristics so that new or uncommon packages are still classified.
acceptanceCriteria: Error handling detector Rust heuristic: if crate imported AND code contains derive(Error) or impl std::error::Error → error handling library
Error handling detector: add known libraries eyre, color-eyre, miette, snafu, error-stack, displaydoc
Error handling detector Python heuristic: class inheriting from name containing Error/Exception → custom exception
Logging detector name-based heuristic: dependency name contains log/logger/logging/trace/tracing/observ → likely logging at lower confidence
Logging detector API shape heuristic: imported module used with .info()/.debug()/.warn()/.error()/.fatal()/.trace() calls → structured logging
Heuristic findings have KnowledgeWeight::Weak or KnowledgeWeight::Info — never Strong or Rule
Known-library findings always take priority over heuristic findings for same package
Unit tests: known library → high confidence, unknown-but-matched → low confidence, no match → no finding
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Independent of registry/trend stories. Enhances existing detectors.

userStories.title: Heuristic fallbacks for testing and dependency usage detectors
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the test patterns and dependency usage detectors to identify unknown libraries via heuristics so that uncommon frameworks and packages are classified.
acceptanceCriteria: Test patterns detector config file detection: jest.config.* → Jest, vitest.config.* → Vitest, [tool.pytest] in pyproject.toml → pytest
Test patterns detector unknown framework fallback: file in test directory with test-prefixed functions but framework unidentified → 'uses testing (framework unknown)'
Test patterns detector dependency name heuristic: name contains test/mock/assert/spec → testing-related

Dependency usage detector name-based heuristic for unrecognized packages: test/mock → Testing, log/trace → Logging, http/web/api/rest → Http, sql/db/orm → Database, cli/command/arg → Cli, serial/json/yaml/proto → Serialization, valid/schema → Validation
Heuristic findings have KnowledgeWeight::Weak or KnowledgeWeight::Info — never Strong or Rule
Known-library findings always take priority over heuristic findings for same package
Unit tests for each heuristic path
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Independent of registry/trend stories. Enhances existing detectors.

userStories.title: Fix dead code: use JavaScriptIR module_system in export detector
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the export patterns detector to read the JavaScriptIR::module_system field and flag mixed ESM/CJS so that this dead code becomes functional.
acceptanceCriteria: Export patterns detector reads JavaScriptIR::module_system field (currently unused dead code) to emit 'project uses ESM/CommonJS/mixed' finding
Flag mixed ESM + CJS in same file as Observation finding
Heuristic findings have KnowledgeWeight::Weak or KnowledgeWeight::Info
Unit tests: pure ESM file, pure CJS file, mixed file → correct findings
cargo test --workspace passes
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings passes
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Branch code review and quality gate
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a comprehensive code review of all changes in the feature branch before merging so that the code meets professional Rust quality standards.
acceptanceCriteria: cargo fmt --all --check — no formatting violations
cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings — no lint warnings
cargo test --workspace — all tests pass
cargo doc --no-deps --document-private-items — documentation builds without warnings
No new unsafe blocks added without justification
No .unwrap() or .expect() in library code (only in tests)
Error handling uses thiserror for library errors, ? for propagation
No unnecessary allocations: &str over String in parameters, &[T] over Vec<T> where ownership not needed
No needless .clone() — each clone justified
Collections pre-allocated with with_capacity() where size known
Single DependencyDomain enum — no remnants of old DependencyCategory or duplicate DependencyDomain
New code respects crate boundaries: seshat-core (types), seshat-scanner (pipeline + registry), seshat-storage (persistence), seshat-detectors (detection)
No SQL in scanner or detectors; no HTTP in detectors or graph
#[tracing::instrument] on new public functions
Tests are deterministic — no real HTTP calls, no reliance on system clock
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Final quality gate. Run after all other stories pass. Full git diff main...HEAD analysis.
